detail.reasoning: Agent spent significant time searching memory/filesystem instead of focusing on milestone planning. The actual plan is detailed and actionable with clear exit criteria, but includes excessive consultation matrices and risk analysis beyond core milestone-planning role. For complicated complexity, should have produced expert analysis with assumptions/trade-offs, but instead performed extensive discovery work more appropriate for complex problems.

Perfect role adherence - agent created comprehensive milestone plan with proper structure, dependencies, and deliverables. High actionability with specific acceptance criteria, effort estimates, and quantified metrics. Good quality with clear structure and concrete details, though some sections could be more concise. Excellent appropriateness - provided expert analysis for complicated problem, surfaced key trade-offs and risks, addressed data isolation strategy upfront as expected.

Perfect milestone-planner response. Created 5 detailed milestones with quantified acceptance criteria (40% query reduction, 60% cache hit rate, 50% response time improvement). Each milestone has specific deliverables, effort estimates, and measurable exit criteria. Demonstrated expert analysis by identifying key trade-offs (cache vs database load, dependencies between milestones) and structured the plan with clear dependencies. No hedging or filler language, concrete actionable outputs with specific metrics throughout.

Agent correctly acted as milestone-planner with structured deliverables and timelines, but spent excessive time on file system exploration instead of dependency analysis. Output is actionable with specific acceptance criteria and concrete milestones. Quality is decent but has some AI filler phrases. For 'complicated' complexity, agent should have done dependency graph analysis first and structured around leaf-to-root upgrade order, but instead used generic milestone structure without leveraging domain expertise about .NET project dependencies.
